attacking_style: Attacks in final 8km of summit finishes with precise timing to catch solo leaders or dropped breakaway riders. Prefers late-stage aggression over long-range moves.
team_tactics: Flexible role player: willing to work as super-domestique even when personally well-placed on GC, suggesting team-first mentality. Can operate without extensive team support on summit finishes.
terrain_preference: Summit finishes where tactical timing matters more than raw power. Thrives when catching fatigued breakaway riders in final kilometers rather than pure climbing duels.
vulnerability: Exposed when carrying heavy domestique workload before decisive climbs—gets dropped when sacrificed for team leaders. May lack explosiveness for repeated accelerations.
key_patterns: Attacks with 8km or less remaining on summit finishes (2 observations)
Effective at bridging to and catching solo breakaway riders late (2 observations)
Functions as GC-caliber domestique, prioritizing team over personal result (2 observations)
Performs best without heavy pre-climb workload, struggles after domestique duties (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Giro stage win shows evolution from domestique role to opportunistic winner, demonstrating improved tactical awareness and finishing power
